home_role: Data Scientist · Ph.D. Student in Statistics
home_desc: I build machine learning systems that survive contact with the real world — and I research why they sometimes don't. My work lives between explainable AI and MLOps: models you can trust, monitor, and actually understand.

about.title: About Me
about.role: Data Scientist | Ph.D. Student in Statistics
about.intro: I'm Ugur — a data scientist and Statistics PhD researcher who likes building things that actually ship. My world sits between research and production: explainable AI and data-drift detection on one side, and the engineering it takes to put models in front of real users on the other. I'm most at home in R — I build full applications with R/Shiny, expose models through plumber APIs, and have authored three CRAN packages: datadriftR, fdicdata, and shinykanban. I also work in Python and across the modern web/cloud stack — Next.js/React, AWS, Firebase, Docker, Fly.io. Along the way I've led a data science team of up to 10, shipped everything from bank-failure prediction models to customer-facing apps, and published 2 peer-reviewed papers.

points: Industry research collaboration on Explainable AI for Time Series and the Rashomon Perspective — post-hoc explanations and sets of near-optimal models for telecom forecasting, aligned with my PhD research agenda.
Led a data science & product team of up to 10, owning ML pipelines end-to-end from data ingestion to production.
Built a weekly automated AWS pipeline ingesting and cleaning FDIC regulatory data across 4,500+ U.S. banks, and a bank-failure prediction model that flagged 2 banks which subsequently failed in 2024.
Designed and built the company website (Next.js/React) and shipped a production analytics Shiny app, owning both frontend and backend in R/Shiny ({rhino}).
Built a scheduled AWS Fargate pipeline that web-scrapes and cleans UCC filing data into Elasticsearch, with CI/CD deployment.
Built R (plumber) REST APIs deployed on Fly.io, and containerized services on AWS (EC2, Fargate) with CI/CD and S3 storage.
Authored CRAN R packages fdicdata and shinykanban, and embedded LLM features in production apps.
Evaluated ML models for marketing analytics and applied predictive imputation to improve data completeness.
Built and queried database tables for marketing data pipelines.

education.detail: Research on explainable AI and concept drift detection — developing partial dependence profile–based methods for explainable drift detection (R package datadriftR, CRAN), with first-author publications and international conference presentations.
Thesis: Detecting Data Drift in Predictive Models Using Explainable AI Tools. Developed the R package datadriftR.
Graduation project: Line-Based Labor Optimization and Decision Support System in a Glass Factory (TUBITAK 2209-B) — Sisecam.
Graduated with first-class honours. Graduation project: Improving Model Performance by Using Explainable AI Tools (TUBITAK 2209-A, BAP-209).

about.hobbies: Outside data science I'm into movies & TV series, biking, and history — and I genuinely love wandering through ancient cities. That passion turned into a side project:
